metadata.actual_document_title: Guidance for Industry: Patient-Reported Outcome Measures: Use in Medical Product Development to Support Labeling Claims
metadata.issuing_body: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Food and Drug Administration
metadata.note: Filename references warnings/precautions labeling; actual document content is FDA PRO Guidance December 2009

rules.rule_text: A PRO instrument used to support a labeling claim must have content validity established through qualitative research in the target population.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A – Content Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: Content validity is the most important measurement property of a PRO instrument because it is the foundation upon which all other measurement properties rest. Without content validity, neither reliability nor other evidence of validity can ensure that the instrument adequately represents the concept of interest.

rules.rule_text: A conceptual framework must be documented for every PRO instrument, defining the relationships between the concept measured, the domains, and the items included.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A – Content Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: A conceptual framework is a description of the relationships between the concept of interest (e.g., symptom, functional status, health-related quality of life), the domains, and the items (i.e., individual questions or observations) included in the instrument.

rules.rule_text: Item generation for PRO instruments must be based on qualitative research conducted with patients from the target population, including focus groups, open-ended interviews, or cognitive interviews.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A – Content Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: Items should be generated from qualitative research, usually involving open-ended interviews and/or focus groups conducted with members of the target patient population.
exceptions: Well-established instruments with existing content validity data may not require new qualitative research

rules.rule_text: Cognitive interviews must be used to evaluate patient comprehension of PRO instrument items, instructions, and response options before finalization.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A – Content Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: Cognitive interviews are used to evaluate whether patients understand the items and response options of the instrument in the way that they were intended.

rules.rule_text: Scoring algorithms for PRO instruments must be empirically justified and documented, including the rationale for domain structure, item weighting, and composite score construction.
rules.source_section: Section IV.B – Instrument Development
rules.verbatim_quote: The method used for scoring the PRO instrument, including any instructions for recoding and missing data handling, should be defined a priori and documentation should be available to support the scoring approach, including rationale for the domain structure and item weighting.

rules.rule_text: Test-retest reliability must be demonstrated for PRO instruments proposed to support labeling claims, using appropriate statistical measures such as intraclass correlation coefficients.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C – Reliability
rules.verbatim_quote: Test-retest reliability (the degree to which an instrument produces stable scores on successive measurements separated by a period of time during which the patient's status is not expected to change) should be documented.
exceptions: Single-administration instruments where retest is conceptually inappropriate

rules.rule_text: Internal consistency reliability must be documented for multi-item PRO instruments, typically using Cronbach's alpha.
rules.source_section: Section IV.C – Reliability
rules.verbatim_quote: Internal consistency reliability (the degree to which items within a domain or scale of an instrument are interrelated) should be documented, generally by Cronbach's coefficient alpha.

rules.rule_text: Construct validity must be demonstrated for PRO instruments through evidence of convergent, discriminant, and known-groups validity.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D – Construct Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: Construct validity includes convergent validity (the relationship between the PRO instrument and other measures of the same or similar concepts), discriminant validity (the ability of the instrument to distinguish between groups known to differ on the concept being measured), and known-groups validity.

rules.rule_text: The ability of a PRO instrument to detect change must be demonstrated when the instrument is intended to measure treatment effects over time.
rules.source_section: Section IV.D – Construct Validity
rules.verbatim_quote: Ability to detect change refers to the ability of the PRO instrument to detect change in the concept of interest when it has occurred.

rules.rule_text: A responder definition — a meaningful within-person change threshold — must be pre-specified and documented when a PRO endpoint is used to support labeling claims.
rules.source_section: Section IV.E – Interpretation of Scores
rules.verbatim_quote: A responder definition, which is a threshold for defining a meaningful within-person change in score (often called a minimally important difference), specific to the clinical trial population, should be developed and used to interpret the clinical meaningfulness of the trial results.

rules.rule_text: When a PRO instrument is modified (e.g., item wording, response options, recall period, new population), the modified version must demonstrate comparable content validity and other measurement properties to the original.
rules.source_section: Section V – Modifying Existing Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: When modifications are made to an existing instrument, evidence that content validity and other measurement properties are comparable between the original and modified instruments (including use in a new indication or population) must be provided.

rules.rule_text: Translated or culturally adapted versions of PRO instruments must provide evidence that content validity and measurement properties are comparable to the original language version.
rules.source_section: Section V – Modifying Existing Instruments / Language Translation
rules.verbatim_quote: Evidence that content validity and other measurement properties are comparable between the original and new instruments should be provided for each translated or culturally adapted version.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials supporting PRO labeling claims must be adequately blinded to minimize bias in patient-reported assessments.
rules.source_section: Section VI – PRO Instruments in Clinical Trials
rules.verbatim_quote: Open-label studies are rarely adequate for supporting PRO labeling claims because they do not minimize potential bias in patient-reported assessments. The clinical trial should be adequately blinded.
exceptions: Conditions where blinding is not feasible; must provide justification

rules.rule_text: Each PRO endpoint must be stated as a specific clinical trial objective in the protocol, with multiplicity concerns addressed.
rules.source_section: Section VI – PRO Instruments in Clinical Trials / Appendix Section XI.A
rules.verbatim_quote: Each PRO endpoint is stated as a specific clinical trial objective and multiplicity concerns are addressed.

rules.rule_text: The protocol must specify that PRO instrument scoring is consistent with the scoring procedures used during instrument development.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Plans for PRO instrument scoring are consistent with those used during instrument development.

rules.rule_text: Plans for handling missing PRO data must be pre-specified in the protocol and the statistical analysis plan.
rules.source_section: Section VI – PRO Instruments in Clinical Trials; Appendix Section XI.A–B
rules.verbatim_quote: Plans are included for handling missing data ... Plans for handling missing data at both the instrument and patient level [must be in the SAP].

rules.rule_text: The statistical analysis plan (SAP) must include plans for multiplicity adjustment when multiple PRO endpoints are tested.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.B – Statistical Analysis Plan
rules.verbatim_quote: Statistical analysis plan (SAP). Ensure the SAP includes: Plans for multiplicity adjustment.

rules.rule_text: The SAP must describe how between-group differences will be portrayed, including cumulative distribution function comparisons.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.B – Statistical Analysis Plan
rules.verbatim_quote: Description of how between-group differences will be portrayed (e.g., cumulative distribution function).

rules.rule_text: When PRO data are collected electronically, evidence must be provided that procedures for maintenance, transmission, and storage of electronic source documents comply with regulatory requirements.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section IX – Data Collection Method
rules.verbatim_quote: If electronic data collection is used to assess PRO endpoints, evidence that procedures for maintenance, transmission, and storage of electronic source documents comply with regulatory requirements.

rules.rule_text: When multiple data collection methods are used, content validity and measurement properties must be shown to be comparable across all methods.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section IX.B – Data Collection Method
rules.verbatim_quote: Evidence that content validity and other measurement properties are comparable among all data collection methods.

rules.rule_text: Proxy-reported outcomes are discouraged for adult patients capable of self-report; observer-reported outcomes are appropriate only when the patient cannot self-report (e.g., very young children, cognitively impaired patients).
rules.source_section: Section III – Types of PRO Instruments
rules.verbatim_quote: When the patient is unable to report (e.g., is too young, too ill, or cognitively impaired), an observer-reported outcome (ObsRO) can provide an alternative. However, this is not preferable when the concept of interest is best assessed by the patient.
exceptions: Pediatric patients under age threshold; patients who cannot self-report due to cognitive impairment or disease severity

rules.rule_text: For pediatric PRO claims, the age appropriateness of items and the developmental capacity of the target population must be specifically established through qualitative research.
rules.source_section: Section VII – Pediatric Considerations
rules.verbatim_quote: The content validity of a PRO instrument intended for use in pediatric populations should be established in the specific pediatric population with consideration of age-appropriate language, cognitive development, and ability to self-report.

rules.rule_text: The frequency and timing of PRO assessments in clinical trials must be appropriate for the patient population, the trial design, and the demonstrated measurement properties of the instrument.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Frequency and timing of PRO assessments are appropriate given patient population, clinical trial design and objectives, and demonstrated PRO measurement properties.

rules.rule_text: The clinical trial duration must be adequate to support PRO objectives, given the nature of the disease and the concept being measured.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical trial duration is adequate to support PRO objectives.

rules.rule_text: Training procedures for patients, interviewers, and clinical investigators on PRO instrument administration must be well-described in the protocol.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Procedures for training are well-described for: Patients, Interviewers, Clinical investigators.

rules.rule_text: Plans for instrument administration must be consistent with the instrument's user manual.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Plans for instrument administration are consistent with instrument's user manual.

rules.rule_text: Procedures must include assessment of PRO endpoint before or shortly after a patient withdraws from the clinical trial.
rules.source_section: Appendix Section XI.A – Clinical Trial Protocol
rules.verbatim_quote: Procedures include assessment of PRO endpoint before or shortly after a patient withdraws from the clinical trial.

rules.rule_text: The FDA PRO Dossier submitted to support a labeling claim must include a full qualitative research summary specifying study type, population characteristics, and full transcripts.
rules.source_section: Appendix – PRO Dossier Section V.E
rules.verbatim_quote: Summary of qualitative studies demonstrating how item pool was generated, reduced, and finalized. Specify type of study (i.e., focus group, patient interview, or cognitive interview) and characteristics of study population. Include full transcripts and datasets in Appendix C.

rules.rule_text: The PRO Dossier must include an item tracking matrix (Appendix B) tracing every item from concept generation through finalization.
rules.source_section: Appendix – PRO Dossier Appendix B
rules.verbatim_quote: Appendix B — Item Tracking Matrix

rules.rule_text: The PRO Dossier must include measurement property evidence covering: reliability (internal consistency and test-retest), construct validity (convergent, discriminant, known-groups), and ability to detect change for each domain or summary score proposed to support claims.
rules.source_section: Appendix – PRO Dossier Section VI.B
rules.verbatim_quote: Summary of testing results for each domain or summary score proposed as support for claims: 1. Reliability (internal; test-retest) 2. Construct validity (convergent, discriminant, known-groups) 3. Ability to detect change.
